text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously bullish - Wells Fargo calling for a summer rally, AI trade showing renewed momentum per headlines. That's tailwind for your chip/tech concentration, but you're mostly in daily-premium territory meaning prices are stretched, not cheap.

Your holdings: SOXL (-7.06%) is the one screaming for attention - it's a 3x leveraged chip ETF, so that -7% on your book is really ~2-2.5% underlying move amplified, and on margin it bites twice. Stop is $200, price is $236 - still room, but watch it closely. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history; tiny position fine, but margin on a fresh IPO is dangerous if it gaps. SMH (-0.69%) is fine but soft; it rhymes with SOXL weakness.

Watch: 1) Whether the AI-trade-powering-higher narrative gets any real follow-through in chip names - SMH and SOXL need it most. 2) SOXL's $200 stop - if chips fade, that level gets real fast on leverage.

Bottom line: Your biggest risk today is SOXL on margin - respect that stop and don't average down into a leveraged decay product.
plays: SOXL ($236.52, -7.06%): No add here. Leveraged decay plus margin is a dangerous combo - let it ride only if above $200 stop; any close threatening that level, cut it.
CRWD: Scanner shows ACCUMULATE 8/12, entry $741-742, stop $740.31 - but that stop is razor-thin (~$1 below entry). Only worth a small add if you want to press a winner; size accordingly.
No new buys today beyond what's noted - most scanner setups are in daily premium (chasing), and forcing trades into stretched levels on margin is how drawdowns happen.
